title: Farm Table — Normalized Task Interface Schema (DRAFT)
description: Draft JSON Schema for the Farm Table task runtime. Defines the Normalized Task Object (NTO) and all supporting entities. Tasks may originate from the built-in graph backend (platform='farmtable') or from external platforms normalized into the same schema.

Platform.description: Task storage backend. 'farmtable' indicates the task is stored natively in the built-in graph backend; all others are external platforms normalized into the NTO.

TaskPhase.description: Tier 1 status: universal lifecycle phase. Every task has exactly one phase. Agents use this for coarse decision-making ('is there work to do?').

TaskStage.description: Tier 2 status: fixed set of finer-grained positions within each phase. Farm Table controls this enum. Agents use this for precise decision-making ('is this task ready to start or still in triage?').

PhaseStageMapping.description: Documents which stages are valid within each phase. Used for validation, not stored per-task.

TaskPriority.description: Normalized urgency level for task prioritization.

RelationshipType.description: Semantic relationship between two tasks. Hierarchy (parent/child) is expressed via parent_task_id, not relationships.

UserType.description: Classification of an actor in the system.

IdentityStatus.description: Lifecycle state for a user or service account identity.

AuthMethod.description: Authentication method used for a linked platform account. MCP_OAUTH: Farm Table as MCP client via OAuth. GITHUB_APP: JWT + installation token. ATLASSIAN_CONNECT: Jira's native app auth (JWT). LOCAL_PROCESS: local subprocess access (e.g., Beads CLI).

CustomFieldType.description: Data type of a custom field value.

User.description: A human, agent, or service account that can be assigned tasks or author changes.

id.description: Stable Farm Table identifier for this user.

remote_id.description: The user's identifier in the source platform.

Relationship.description: A directed semantic relationship between two tasks.

target_task_id.description: Farm Table ID of the related task.

CustomFieldValue.description: A value for a platform-specific or organization-defined custom field. Preserves non-standard metadata that does not map to NTO built-in fields.

value.description: The field value. Type varies based on field_type.

Attachment.description: A file attached to a task or comment.

Comment.description: A message in the conversation thread of a task. Body is normalized to Markdown regardless of source platform format.

remote_id.description: Comment identifier in the source platform.

Change.description: A field-level diff recording how a task was modified. Forms a tamper-proof audit trail of all agent and human actions.

field.description: Name of the field that changed (e.g., 'phase', 'stage', 'assignees', 'priority').

reason.description: Optional explanation for the change, supporting the 'auditable by construction' principle.

StatusMapping.description: Maps a platform-native status label to a Farm Table normalized phase and stage.

native_status.description: The status label as it appears in the source platform.

LinkedAccount.description: A connection between a Farm Table deployment and a platform account. Farm Table acts as an auth bridge: agents authenticate only to Farm Table, and Farm Table uses linked accounts to proxy operations to external platforms.

scopes.description: Granted permission scopes (least-privilege set for current work).

remote_user_id.description: User or bot identifier in the source platform.

CodeContext.description: Typed domain extension for code-related task context. Provides structured fields that coding agents need to begin work, rather than requiring agents to parse remote_data.

branch.description: Target or working branch for this task.

pull_requests.description: Pull requests linked to this task.

ci_status.description: Aggregate CI/CD pipeline status for the task's associated code changes.

commit_shas.description: Relevant commit SHAs associated with this task.

Collection.description: A grouping of tasks representing a project, board, or repository. Each collection maps 1:1 to a single external platform integration. Defines the operational boundary for agent work and scopes task queries.

id.description: Stable Farm Table identifier for this collection.

remote_id.description: Identifier in the source platform (e.g., repo full name, project key, team ID). Null for collections in the built-in backend.

workspace_id.description: Platform workspace or organization identifier, if applicable.

linked_account_id.description: Reference to the LinkedAccount used to authenticate with this collection's platform.

status_mappings.description: Per-collection mapping of native statuses to normalized phase and stage. Supports custom workflows (e.g., Jira's configurable status schemes).

custom_field_definitions.description: Custom fields available in this collection, discovered via platform API.

remote_data.description: Raw platform-specific metadata for this collection.

Task.description: The Normalized Task Object (NTO) — the central entity of Farm Table. Encapsulates all information an agent needs to understand and execute a piece of work, regardless of source platform.

id.description: Stable Farm Table identifier. Globally unique across all platforms.

name.description: Title or summary of the task.

description.description: Detailed work instructions, normalized to Markdown.

acceptance_criteria.description: Conditions that must be met for this task to be considered complete, normalized to Markdown. First-class field in the built-in backend; for external platforms, may be extracted from structured description sections or dedicated fields (e.g., Beads' acceptance_criteria).

stage.description: Tier 2 status: finer-grained position within the phase.

native_status.description: Tier 3 status: the original status label from the source platform, preserved verbatim for round-trip fidelity.

type.description: The kind of work this task represents (e.g., 'epic', 'story', 'task', 'bug', 'subtask'). Freeform string to accommodate platform-specific types (Jira custom issue types, Beads issue_type, Asana resource_subtype). Null when the source platform has no task typing.

priority.description: Normalized urgency level. Null when the source platform has no native priority (e.g., GitHub Issues).

assignees.description: Agents and/or humans assigned to this task.

creator.description: The user who created the task.

start_date.description: ISO 8601 planned start date. Enables agents to reason about work windows and scheduling.

collection_id.description: The collection (project/board/repo) this task belongs to.

parent_task_id.description: Reference to a higher-level task in the hierarchy (supports up to 8 levels per GitHub sub-issues).

relationships.description: Non-hierarchical dependency and semantic links to other tasks (blocks, blocked_by, related, duplicate). Hierarchy is expressed via parent_task_id.

labels.description: Tags or labels applied to the task.

code_context.description: Typed domain extension for code-related context. Present when the task is associated with code work (repos, branches, PRs, CI). Null for non-code tasks.

remote_id.description: Identifier in the source platform (e.g., GitHub issue number, Jira key PROJ-123, Linear UUID). Null for tasks in the built-in backend.

remote_url.description: Web URL to the task in the source platform.

remote_data.description: Raw, un-transformed payload from the source platform. Escape hatch for fields that do not map to the NTO or typed extensions.

closed_at.description: Timestamp when the task entered the CLOSED phase. Null while the task is open. Essential for cycle-time metrics and agent performance analysis.

WebhookEvent.description: A normalized event emitted when a task changes in any connected platform. Supports both native webhooks and virtual webhooks (poll-derived) for platforms without webhook support.

changes.description: Field-level diffs included with this event.

source.description: Whether this event came from a platform webhook or was synthesized by polling.

description: Root schema object. A Farm Table document may contain any combination of the defined entities.
